comments.author: vp
comments.text:
Verification audit (no code changes) for inactive performer filtering + historical visibility.

Scope checked
- Backend lifecycle filter + include_ids passthrough:
  - backend/src/services/performerLifecycle.ts
  - backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/persons.ts (`/persons/list_performers`)
  - backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions.ts (`/auth/list-users`)
  - backend/src/api/routes/crm/dictionary.ts
- Frontend selector consumers:
  - app/src/utils/performerLifecycle.ts
  - app/src/components/crm/CRMCreateTicket.tsx
  - app/src/components/crm/CRMKanban.tsx
  - app/src/components/crm/WorkHoursSidebar.tsx
  - app/src/components/voice/PossibleTasks.tsx
  - app/src/components/voice/AccessUsersModal.tsx
  - app/src/store/voiceBotStore.ts

Evidence run (targeted tests)
- backend:
  - npm test -- __tests__/services/performerLifecycle.test.ts __tests__/voicebot/personsListPerformersRoute.test.ts __tests__/voicebot/authListUsersRoute.test.ts __tests__/api/crmDictionaryPerformerLifecycleContract.test.ts
  - result: 4/4 suites passed, 6/6 tests passed
- app:
  - npm test -- __tests__/operops/crmPerformerLifecycleSelectorsContract.test.ts __tests__/voice/possibleTasksPerformerLifecycleContract.test.ts __tests__/voice/accessUsersPerformerLifecycleContract.test.ts __tests__/voice/performerHiddenSelectorsContract.test.ts
  - result: 4/4 suites passed, 11/11 tests passed

Findings
1) Confirmed: canonical lifecycle filtering is implemented for active selectors.
- Backend selector filter enforces `is_deleted != true`, `is_active != false`, `active != false`, and hidden denylist clauses.
- `include_ids` is explicitly OR-ed to keep historical assignees visible/selectable when already referenced.

2) Confirmed: historical visibility behavior is implemented across CRM + Voice selectors.
- CRM Create Ticket, Kanban inline edit, WorkHours sidebar: inactive/deleted performers are excluded unless historical for current context.
- Voice Possible Tasks and Access Users: include historical ids in fetch payload + keep historical rows visible while keeping inactive hidden in add-selector flows.

3) Gap/risk (parity): frontend hidden-denylist utility does not check `real_name`, backend filter does.
- Backend denylist includes `real_name`.
- Frontend `isPerformerSelectable` checks `name/username/telegram_username/login`, but not `real_name` directly.
- If a data source provides denylisted alias only via `real_name` without mapping to `name`, frontend could diverge from backend rule.

4) Gap/risk (mode behavior): CRM dictionary route with `show_inactive=true` bypasses lifecycle selector filter entirely.
- Current default `isInActiveVisible=false` and no active UI setter usage found, so default path is safe.
- If this flag is later surfaced/used, performer selector lists may include inactive/deleted not tied to historical assignments.

Proposal notes (no code change in this ticket)
- Keep issue open and treat as contract-hardening follow-up.
- Add a parity guard test that asserts frontend lifecycle utility fields stay aligned with backend filter clauses (especially `real_name`).
- Add a route-level contract test for CRM dictionary `show_inactive=true` clarifying intended selector behavior (strict historical-only vs explicit broad include).
- If product intent remains “hide inactive in selectors except historical,” consider decoupling performer selector source from broad `show_inactive` dictionary mode.

title: Скрывать Unknown в категоризации как неинформативное значение
description: Проверить правило показа speaker=Unknown (скрывать по умолчанию, показывать только при отличном значении).
notes:
Verification-only: do not change code in this ticket; prepare proposal and validation notes.
Proposal: suppress speaker label Unknown в категоризации, отображать только meaningful values.
Audit 2026-02-28 (verification-only, no code changes):

Findings:
1) Unknown suppression in categorization row UI is implemented now.
- app/src/components/voice/CategorizationTableRow.tsx:16 normalizes rawSpeakerLabel=='unknown' to empty string in metadata signature path.
- app/src/components/voice/CategorizationTableRow.tsx:50 hides visible speaker label when speakerLabel.toLowerCase()=="unknown".

2) Backend still stores speaker='Unknown' as canonical fallback (expected for data consistency).
- backend/src/workers/voicebot/handlers/categorize.ts:266 uses normalizeString(item.speaker, 'Unknown').
- This means suppression is presentation-level (frontend), not data deletion.

3) Focused verification tests passed:
- app: __tests__/voice/categorizationUnknownSpeakerContract.test.ts
- app: __tests__/voice/categorizationMetadataSignatureContract.test.ts
- backend: __tests__/voicebot/workerCategorizeHandler.test.ts

Proposal / risk note:
- Avatar derivation in app/src/store/voiceBotStore.ts:490 checks only exact 'Unknown' (case-sensitive, without trim).
- For legacy/model variants like 'unknown', 'UNKNOWN', or ' Unknown ', label is hidden in UI row but avatar derivation may still produce non-informative glyphs (e.g. 'U' or whitespace-derived char).
- Proposed follow-up: normalize speaker once via trim().toLowerCase() in avatar mapping, and treat unknown-equivalent values as non-meaningful consistently in both label and avatar branches.

Status: keeping issue open for review/approval and potential implementation ticket.
Audit addendum (human-readable):
- UI suppression for speaker=Unknown is present in categorization row rendering and metadata signature.
- Backend intentionally persists Unknown as fallback speaker value; suppression is presentation-only.

  - `d11e757f-2073-4f1d-919d-4c4c11dd0eab`
  - `457b47f0-4678-4367-ab70-05d85a849f68`
  - `3e21be5e-9c04-4ab9-b528-58053c059f6c`

Findings: referenced chunk families
- `002-1.webm` (latest sampled docs): reproducible missing timing (`start/end=""`) in recent rows, including:
  - message `2683b26f-1f55-4708-a568-f4498f3b2ca9` (2026-02-28, runtime_tag=`prod-p2`)
  - message `060ad405-1f7d-4541-832b-ed702f2f0c59` (2026-02-27, runtime_tag=`prod-p2`)
- `009-*`: mixed state; some rows canonical clock, some empty, e.g.
  - `009-2.webm` message `681e70ac-f8c6-4356-a960-0e16eb25afc3` -> clock format
  - `009-1.webm` message `3e843dd2-96e2-4786-8fa4-e2dde64425db` -> empty format
- `009-01.webm`: no direct matches found (pattern absent in sampled documents).
- `full-mic*-seg*`: mixed state; some clock, some empty, e.g.
  - `full-mic1-seg013.webm` message `336bf29e-e8ff-470b-bc40-8ef37a01c5dc` -> empty
  - `full-mic2-seg008.webm` message `1550c690-d944-43a7-a1d3-49e1b28f695a` -> clock

Cross-check against current code/runtime
- Source code (`backend/src/workers/voicebot/handlers/categorize.ts`) and built artifact (`backend/dist/.../categorize.js`) already contain timeline normalization to `MM:SS/HH:MM:SS` with non-empty fallback (`00:00`).
- Active PM2 worker `copilot-voicebot-workers-prod` has uptime older than latest categorize timestamp-hardening commit/build (process started earlier and not restarted after latest dist rebuild).

Likely root cause
1) Historical rows in session `699ec607...` were generated before timestamp hardening was applied in runtime.
2) Additional recent bad rows in `prod-p2` strongly suggest at least one active worker/runtime path still executes older categorization logic (or stale process state predating normalization rollout).
3) `transcription.segments` in affected rows frequently have `start=0/end=0`, so even with normalization absent/partial pipeline degrades to empty/raw model timing values.

Proposed fix strategy (implementation proposal, not applied here)
1) Operational rollout: rebuild and restart all voice worker runtimes that process prod-family queues, then verify new categorizations store canonical non-empty timing.
2) Data backfill: one-shot migration for affected rows (`start/end in ["", null]` or raw numeric string) to canonical labels, preserving monotonic ranges.
3) Add runtime guardrail metric/log: count writes with empty timing and alert when >0 to prevent silent regressions.
4) Validation pass: recheck `699ec607...` and targeted chunk families (`002-1`, `009-*`, `full-mic*`) after rollout+backfill.
close_reason: Verification completed: audit notes/proposal recorded, no code-change scope in this issue.

title: Колонка Materials вместо Quick Summary + связывание image+text
description: Проверить data-model и UI-контракт для связки изображения и следующего текста в одной логической строке.
notes:
Verification-only: do not change code in this ticket; prepare proposal and validation notes.
Proposal: колонка Materials с привязкой image+following text через cross-links в data model.
Decision (approved): Materials relation model is one-to-many (one material can link to multiple rows).
Audit update (verification-only, no code changes)
Date: 2026-02-28

Scope audited
- Materials column contract in Voice Categorization UI.
- image+text linking model (explicit target link + implicit next-message anchor flow).
- Alignment with approved decision: one material -> many rows.

Evidence checked
- Frontend header/rows:
  - app/src/components/voice/CategorizationTableHeader.tsx
  - app/src/components/voice/CategorizationTableRow.tsx
  - app/src/components/voice/Categorization.tsx
  - app/src/store/voiceBotStore.ts
  - app/src/pages/voice/SessionPage.tsx
  - app/src/types/voice.ts
- Backend routes/realtime:
  - backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions.ts
  - backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/uploads.ts
- Contract tests (all PASS):
  - app/__tests__/voice/categorizationColumnsContract.test.ts
  - app/__tests__/voice/voiceImageAnchorGroupingContract.test.ts
  - app/__tests__/voice/rowMaterialTargetSelectionContract.test.ts
  - backend/__tests__/voicebot/rowMaterialTargetRouteContract.test.ts

Current implementation summary
1) Materials column
- Quick Summary is removed from Categorization; header renders "Materials".
- Rows render image preview in Materials cell when row.imageUrl exists.

2) Explicit image->target-row linking (manual)
- User selects a target row (Transcription/Categorization) via sessionsUIStore.materialTargetMessageId.
- Paste flow forwards targetMessageId from SessionPage to addSessionImageChunk.
- addSessionImageChunk sends image_anchor_linked_message_id in POST voicebot/add_text payload.
- Backend validates target belongs to same session and stores image_anchor_linked_message_id on inserted message.
- Realtime new_message emits image_anchor_linked_message_id.

3) Implicit image->next-text linking (pending anchor)
- On image add, session stores pending_image_anchor_*.
- First next audio upload consumes pending anchor and writes image_anchor_message_id on created voice message.
- Pending marker is unset after consumption.

4) Frontend grouping/material cross-links
- transformVoiceBotMessagesToGroups reconstructs relation in client:
  - linkedAnchorRows by image_anchor_message_id
  - explicitLinkedRows by image_anchor_linked_message_id
- Synthetic fields are added in UI model: material_group_id, material_anchor_message_id, material_target_message_id, material_source_message_id.

Gap analysis vs approved one-to-many model
G1. Persisted model is effectively one-anchor->zero/one explicit target.
- Backend stores only singular image_anchor_linked_message_id on anchor message.
- One material cannot natively persist links to multiple target rows without duplicating anchor messages.

G2. material_* relation fields are UI-derived, not canonical backend contract.
- No backend persistence/emission for material_group_id/material_anchor_message_id/material_target_message_id.
- Cross-client consistency depends on each client re-running local reconstruction logic.

G3. Group-level material ids collapse multi-anchor cases.
- In target group assembly, explicitLinkedEntries can contain multiple anchors, but materialAnchorMessageId takes first entry only.
- This can misrepresent anchor identity at group metadata level.

G4. No dedicated relation lifecycle API.
- No explicit link/unlink/update endpoints for material relations.
- Current mutation path is indirect (create anchor message with a single linked target id).

Proposed data contract (for implementation phase)
A. Canonical relation entity (server-owned)
- Add collection (suggested): automation_voice_bot_material_relations
- Record shape:
  - _id: ObjectId
  - session_id: ObjectId
  - runtime_tag: string
  - material_message_id: string (anchor message _id/message_id normalized)
  - target_message_id: string (target message _id/message_id normalized)
  - relation_kind: 'explicit' | 'implicit_next_audio'
  - status: 'active' | 'deleted'
  - created_at, updated_at, deleted_at?
  - created_by?
- One-to-many is expressed as multiple rows with same material_message_id and different target_message_id.

B. Session API response additions
- In /voicebot/get_session payload, include canonical relation block:
  - material_relations: MaterialRelation[]
- Optionally include denormalized material_index for UI efficiency:
  - materials_by_target: { [target_message_id]: MaterialRef[] }

C. Backward compatibility
- Keep image_anchor_linked_message_id for legacy clients during migration.
- Server should write both:
  - legacy singular field (first/primary target)
  - canonical relation rows.
- Mark legacy field as deprecated in route/types docs after cutover.

D. Validation invariants
- relation.session_id must match both anchor and target message session.
- No duplicate active relation for same (material_message_id, target_message_id).
- Soft-delete relation rows instead of hard delete to preserve audit history.

E. Minimum API surface
- POST /api/voicebot/materials/link { session_id, material_message_id, target_message_id }
- POST /api/voicebot/materials/unlink { session_id, material_message_id, target_message_id }
- Optional batch variant for multi-target linking in one call.

Status recommendation
- Keep issue open (as requested).
- This ticket is ready for review/approval of contract before implementation tickets.
close_reason: Verification completed: audit notes/proposal recorded, no code-change scope in this issue.

title: Явный выбор ячейки Materials для загрузки в конкретный чанк
description: Проверить UX и data-flow явного таргетинга загрузки материала в выбранную строку транскрипции/категоризации.
notes:
Verification-only: do not change code in this ticket; prepare proposal and validation notes.
Proposal: explicit material target selection per row (transcription/categorization) с persist + realtime update.
Decision (approved): explicit material targeting should support one-to-many links.
[2026-02-28 audit] Verification-only audit (no code changes): explicit row-targeted material upload UX/data-flow in transcription/categorization.

Scope checked:
- Frontend: app/src/pages/voice/SessionPage.tsx, app/src/store/sessionsUIStore.ts, app/src/store/voiceBotStore.ts, app/src/components/voice/{TranscriptionTableRow,CategorizationTableRow,Categorization}.tsx
- Backend: backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/sessions.ts, backend/src/api/routes/voicebot/uploads.ts
- Contracts: app/__tests__/voice/{rowMaterialTargetSelectionContract,voiceImageAnchorGroupingContract}.test.ts, backend/__tests__/voicebot/rowMaterialTargetRouteContract.test.ts

Validation evidence:
- app tests PASS: rowMaterialTargetSelectionContract, voiceImageAnchorGroupingContract
- backend tests PASS: rowMaterialTargetRouteContract

Findings (current behavior):
1) Target selection exists, but it is message-level (not chunk-level).
- UI stores a single materialTargetMessageId in sessionsUIStore.
- Transcription row click/categorization row click toggles target by row.message_id (or _id fallback), then paste flow forwards targetMessageId.
- Backend accepts image_anchor_linked_message_id and validates target is in-session.

2) Upload trigger is global paste-only from SessionPage.
- There is no explicit per-row Materials upload affordance (button/dropzone in row cell).
- In categorization, row click both sets material target and toggles task selection, coupling two unrelated actions.

3) Explicit Materials cell targeting for a concrete chunk is not implemented.
- Target payload includes only image_anchor_linked_message_id (single message ref).
- No segment-level identifier (segment_oid/time range/categorization row key) is sent or persisted.

4) One-to-many link capability is not represented in storage contract.
- Current anchor link field is singular (image_anchor_linked_message_id).
- A single uploaded material message can point to at most one target message.

5) Cross-tab rendering is asymmetric.
- Categorization groups linked image rows with target group via transformVoiceBotMessagesToGroups.
- Transcription tab remains message-row based; linked material appears as separate image message rather than attached to a specific target chunk row.

Proposal (implementation direction, no code in this ticket):
A) Data contract
- Introduce canonical material_targets: [{ message_ref, segment_oid?, categorization_row_key? }].
- Keep image_anchor_linked_message_id as backward-compatible alias during migration.
- Support one material -> many targets by allowing array cardinality > 1.

B) API
- Extend add_text/add_attachment to accept targets[] and validate each target belongs to session and is not deleted.
- Persist normalized refs (ObjectId/message_id) and return resolved target refs in response.

C) UX
- Add explicit Materials action per row/segment (button or dropzone in target cell).
- Decouple target selection from categorization task checkbox selection.
- Show active target badge/pill with clear action; keep highlight deterministic.

D) Realtime
- Emit new_message with material_targets.
- Emit message_update for each impacted target message so both tabs re-render linked materials without full refresh.

E) Rendering
- In transcription/categorization, render linked materials by target descriptor (message + optional segment).
- Hide standalone anchor-only rows when their materials are fully represented in target rows.

Suggested acceptance checks for follow-up implementation:
- Selecting a specific transcription segment and uploading material attaches to that segment (not just message).
- Selecting multiple targets for one upload reflects all links in UI and persisted payload.
- Categorization checkbox selection does not change upload target unless explicit Materials action is used.
- Realtime updates reflect links in both tabs without manual reload.
close_reason: Verification completed: audit notes/proposal recorded, no code-change scope in this issue.

title: Контракт worker-dispatcher по дочерним bd issues (fail-fast)
description: Единая карточка для фиксации проектных решений и статуса исполнения по будущему worker, который распределяет работу по дочерним тикетам. На этом этапе только контракт и правила, без реализации.
notes:
Scope: contract-only, no implementation.
Decision: remove fallback behavior; fail-fast with explicit error state and retry metadata.
Tracking: use this issue as single source for decision log and execution status updates.
Required sections for updates: [Decision], [Status], [Next checkpoint], [Risks].
[Decision]
Contract v0 approved: worker is fail-fast only (no fallback paths).

[Status]
Contract drafted, implementation not started.

[Contract v0]
1) Goal: distribute work from parent bd issue to child issues and track execution state.
2) Input: parent_issue_id (required), allowed_child_statuses (default: open|in_progress), max_children_per_run (default: 25), run_id.
3) Selection: read children by parent-child dependency, deterministic order by priority asc then created_at asc then id asc.
4) Dispatch: one child -> one dispatch record; duplicates prevented by idempotency key <run_id>:<child_id>.
5) Fail-fast: any contract violation or command error stops current run, marks run failed, writes explicit error_code/error_message; no fallback logic.
6) State model: queued -> dispatched -> in_progress -> done | failed | blocked; state transitions are explicit and monotonic.
7) Retry policy: retries allowed only for transient errors with retry_count and next_retry_at; terminal errors are not auto-retried.
8) Observability: every run writes summary counters (selected/dispatched/succeeded/failed/skipped) + per-child error details.
9) Output: run_result {ok, run_id, parent_issue_id, counters, failed_children[], blocked_children[]}.
10) Out-of-scope (phase 0): no auto-code execution, no auto-close of issues, no status mutation outside worker-owned fields.

[Next checkpoint]
Finalize data schema (run log + per-child execution record).

[Risks]
Missing canonical mapping between task records and bd issue ids can cause ambiguous dispatch targets.

title: [voice][ui] stop rendering per-segment file/timestamp signatures inline as transcript content
description:
Problem
Transcription UI for recovered multi-segment voice messages renders timeline/file signature lines after nearly every segment, e.g. `0:00 - 0:47, Запись встречи 30.03.2026 15-08-25 - запись.webm, 15:16:15`. Operators experience these as content pollution inside the transcript.

Forensic target
- session_id: 69ca581bbe02d5de23661e1b
- URL: https://copilot.stratospace.fun/voice/session/69ca581bbe02d5de23661e1b

Evidence
- Live UI screenshots show the signature repeated between transcript paragraphs on the Transcription tab.
- Session payload shows message rows contain structured `transcription.segments[]` with clean text chunks; the signature string is not persisted in segment text.
- `app/src/components/voice/TranscriptionTableRow.tsx` formats every segment footer via `formatSegmentTimeline()` -> `formatVoiceMetadataSignature(...)`, combining start/end, source filename, and absolute timestamp into a visible line under each segment.
- This produces an operator-facing artifact that looks like content injected into the transcription, especially for long recordings segmented every ~40-50s.

Expected
- Raw transcription content remains readable without repeated metadata signatures mixed into the reading flow.
